rulings.Case Title: BROSNAN PLUMBING VS. THOMAS COLL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Ppursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 2, PLAINTIFF PATRICK BROSNAN, DBA BROSNAN PLUMBING's Motion To Enforce Settlement And For Entry Of Judgment Pursuant To Terms Of Settlement Agreement. Granted. Judgment shall be entered against Defendant in the amount of $46,763.41 pursuant to the terms of the parties' Settlement Agreement. The Settlement Agreement provides that default can be evidenced by "a Declaration of Counsel for [Plaintiff] certifying under penalty of perjury that an uncured default has occurred and the amount thereof, calculated under the provisions hereof." (Declaration of Douglas Akay, Ex. A, Settlement Agreement, par. 6.) The Settlement Agreement further provides for a 10% interest rate. (Id.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: NALL & COMPANY, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. RSP PORTRERO GROUP, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Re: Discovery Order; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT RUTHANN PIETROFESO's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Re: Discovery Order. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition has been filed; good cause is shown for the requested order and the Court will sign the proposed order submitted with the motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants' Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Nos. 104, 105, 111, 118, 124, And 125
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 6, PLAINTIFF JULIE WALKER's Motion To Compel Defendants' Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, Nos. 104, 105, 111, 118, 124, And 125. Motion to Compel further responses is granted in part and denied in part. Specifically, as to Requests 104, 105, 111, 118, 124, and 125, Defendant is ordered to serve verified supplemental responses and produce documents no later than January 30, 2015. If defendant has no further documents, then the response should so state. Further, as to Request 111, Defendants are ordered to produce all responsive documents for copying at the PG&E Chico office no later than January 30, 2015. These responsive documents are to be produced subject to the protective order and are only to be used for the purposes of the present litigation. All copies of these documents are to be returned to Defendant PG&E within 90 days of the conclusion of this litigation and when all appeals have been exhausted. As to Request 118, Defendants are ordered to provide, subject to the protective order, information sufficient to show whether each employee who was the subject of the "employee performance records" produced by Defendants, Bates Numbered DEF005205-5211, was (1) male or female, and (2) 40 years of age or older. Finally, as to Request 124, Defendants are ordered to unseal all responsive documents and produce them to Plaintiff subject to the protective order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Depositions And To Quash Pltff'S Untimely Deposition Notices And Deposition Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANTS DANNY BUIST, PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY's Motion To Stay Depositions And To Quash Plaintiff's Untimely Deposition Notices And Deposition Subpoenas. Motion to Stay is denied as there is no reason to stay the depositions. Motion to Quash Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoena for Production of Business Records is denied as the documents requested are relevant to the litigation and FRASCO did not contest the subpoena. Motions to Quash Plaintiff's Deposition Subpoenas for Personal Appearance to Dale Ulsh and Jim Faulbaum are granted due to Plaintiff's failure to serve an accompanying "notice to consumer or employee and objection" form per CCP §1985.6. However, if Plaintiff properly re-issues and serves deposition subpoenas for personal appearance and an accompanying notices to consumer or employee and objection to Ulsh and Faulbaum, then Defendants shall not have standing to challenge the subpoena as this Motion is denied solely on procedural grounds and the deposition notice is relevant on the merits. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PHILLIP COLLINS et al VS. HAYLEE YEE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Phillip Collins, Genesio Lobato, Thomas Mcvitty And Gary Cheatle'S Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records And Or For Protective Order; Requests For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 5, PLAINTIFFS PHILLIP COLLINS, GENESIO LOBATO, THOMAS MCVITTY AND GARY CHEATLE'S Motion To Quash Defendants Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records And Or For Protective Order; Requests For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiffs' Motion to Quash Defendants' Deposition Subpoenas directed to various charitable and governmental agencies is granted as to all outstanding subpoenas with the exception of those issued on December 18, 2014 as exemplified by Exhibit "A" to the declaration of Paul Sheng. As to those subpoenas, the Motion is granted as to paragraph no. 1 of the request for production of documents as being too broadly worded, and as to paragraph no. 4 of the request for production of documents as it calls for private information not relevant to the issues in the litigation. The Court will assess sanctions in the amount of $1,000.00 to be paid to Plaintiffs' counsel within ten (10) days from the date of this order. Counsel for Plaintiffs should prepare an appropriate order, submit it to counsel for Defendants and deliver it to the court for signing at the hearing on January 28, 2015. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN F GREENWALD et al VS. STEVEN A MINISINI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Steven Minisini And Jennifer Chow'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Directed To Pacific Union International, Inc.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANTS STEVEN MINISINI AND JENNIFER CHOW'S Motion To Quash Subpoena Directed To Pacific Union International, Inc. Pro Tem Judge Paul Renne, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Steven A. Minisini and Jennifer T. Chow's Motion to Quash Subpoena directed to Pacific Union International, Inc. is denied assuming that Plaintiff is agreeable to deleting from the deposition subpoena address to Pacific Union the following language in paragraph 1, second line of attachment 3: "but not limited to ". With the exception of that broad request all items sought by the deposition subpoena would appear to be relevant and would not constitute private financial information of Defendants or any third party. No monetary sanctions were sought and none will be imposed. Counsel for Plaintiff should prepare a proposed order, submit to counsel for Defendants for approval as to form and deliver to the Court for signing at the hearing. Counsel for Plaintiff is also ordered to prepare and serve a revised deposition subpoena directed to Pacific Union conforming with this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to rennepar85@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: GERRIE SCOTT VS. TOM MYERS, AKA TOM MEYERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS TOM MYERS, ANGEL LI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The court sustains the demurrer to the neg per se cause of action without leave to amend. First, sec. 810A(a) of the SF Public Works Code requires the tree to be within 10 feet of a public right-of-way for the permit requirement to apply. Plaintiff admits that the trees that fall under the ordinance were not harmed. (Plaintiff's Opposition to the Demurrer, 5:12-20). Second, neg per se is an evidentiary presumption, not a distinct c/a. See Das v. Bank of America, N.A., (2010) 186 Cal.App.4th 727, 737-738. The court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress and intrusion causes of action. Those claims involve factual issues and defendant fails to show that they fail as a matter of law on demurrer. The demurrer to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. First, the claim is negligence and negligent infliction of emotional distress is technically not a separate cause of action. See Potter v. Firestone Tire and Rubber. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 984. Second, plaintiff cannot recover emotional distress damages on this claim because there was no physical injury or preexisting relationship between the parties to warrant that relief. See Cooper v. Sup. Crt. (1984) 153 Cal.App.3d 1008, 1012. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GERRIE SCOTT VS. TOM MYERS, AKA TOM MEYERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS TOM MYERS, ANGEL LI MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The court grants and denies the motion to strike as follows: A. The prayer for over $25,000. Deny. There is nothing improper as to this request. B. The allegations regarding tree topping and illegality. Grant with leave to amend so as to conform with the demurrer ruling. C. The allegations regarding intrusion. Deny. D. The prayer for actual damages. Deny. E. The prayer for tree replacement. Deny. The prayer is not necessarily improper because plaintiff is seeking restoration. F. The prayer for punitive damages. Deny. The facts support the claim. G. The prayer for attorney's fees. Grant without leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to cite a statutory or contractual basis for fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: MARK HOOSHMAND VS. KATHARINA WOHL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Disqualify Defendants Counsel, Christopher Wohl And The Law Firm Of Palmer Kazajian Wohl Hodson Llp
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, January 28, 2015, Line 10, PLAINTIFF MARK HOOSHMAND's Motion To Disqualify Defendants' Counsel, Christopher Wohl And The Law Firm Of Palmer Kazajian Wohl Hodson LLP. Denied. A party is entitled to his or her choice of counsel and "neither his opponent nor the trial court should be able to deny this choice to the party without a convincing demonstration of detriment to the opponent or injury to the integrity of the judicial process." (Smith, Smith, & Kring v. Sup. Ct. (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 573, 579; see also Maxwell v. Superior Court (1982) 30 Cal.3d 606, 619, fn. 9.) Plaintiff does not convincingly demonstrate detriment to himself or injury to the integrity of the judicial process. Plaintiff does not specifically identify the testimony that it seeks to elicit from Defendant's counsel and does not explain why this testimony cannot be provided by the Defendant. The Declaration of Christopher Wohl establishes that Mr. Wohl did not become involved in the matter until he was substituted in as counsel, and therefore, any communications with Defendant would be protected by the attorney-client privilege. (Wohl Decl., par. 11.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
